topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music swelled, a vibrant wave washing over the crowded ballroom. I found myself leaning against a marble pillar, nursing a lukewarm champagne, watching the joyous chaos unfold. Then, across the room, a familiar silhouette caught my eye. It was Sarah. She was laughing, her head tilted back, the sunlight catching the auburn highlights in her hair. My breath hitched, a small, involuntary catch in my chest. A warmth spread through my core, easing the tightness I hadn't realized I was holding. I watched as she turned, and for a fleeting second, our eyes met. A slow, genuine smile spread across my face, a feeling of profound peace settling over me. I straightened up, a lightness in my step I hadn't possessed all evening.

[story 2]

The air in the reception hall was thick with the scent of lilies and celebration. I was trying to appear casual, sipping my sparkling water, but every time I glanced toward the entrance, my focus snagged on him. David. He was standing near the buffet, talking animatedly with someone, and the way he gestured, the easy confidence in his posture—it was all so familiar, yet somehow different now. When he looked up and saw me, the conversation faltered for a beat. A genuine, unguarded smile broke across my features, and I felt a distinct softening in my shoulders. I pushed off the wall, moving toward the dance floor, my stride feeling unexpectedly buoyant. It was a quiet acknowledgment, a deep sense of contentment settling in my chest, as if a heavy stone had finally lifted.

[story 3]

I stood near the edge of the terrace, the city lights glittering below like scattered diamonds. The noise of the wedding faded into a distant hum. Then, there he was, standing near the railing, looking out over the expanse. It was Alex. He caught my gaze, and the usual guardedness I carried seemed to dissolve instantly. I didn't say anything immediately; instead, I let my eyes linger, a soft, appreciative look passing between us. A subtle shift occurred within me—a relaxation of the jaw, a quiet expansion in my chest. I took a slow, deep breath of the cool night air. A profound sense of rightness washed over me, a deep satisfaction that settled comfortably in my bones. I turned away, a subtle, knowing upturn of my lips indicating everything without needing a single word.
